page: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It also includes 1890 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core tests, while PassMark CPU, physics, and encryption tests rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is solid, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25% and uncached large-block read/write bandwidths of 107.09 GB/sec and 72.68 GB/sec respectively. For lightweight AI workloads, it achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 404.59 tokens/sec on a small 135M LLM model.

Given its low-memory profile, this server offers a cost-effective option for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage offsets external storage requirements, enhancing qualitative cost efficiency for localized data processing. However, the 64.0 GB RAM limit may act as a bottleneck for memory-intensive database operations, which perform at average levels in PassMark database and Redis SET operations. This configuration is highly suitable for single-threaded processing, static web serving, lightweight LLM inference, and compute-bound workloads like compression and physics simulations.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized, low-memory server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage. The server lacks GPU acceleration and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with average tiers. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for compute-intensive, single-threaded tasks, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference, though the limited memory capacity presents a tradeoff for large-scale database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 64.0 GB RAM and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for high-frequency single-core workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, low-memory compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and substantial local storage for single-threaded and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU, compression, and decompression performance
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight LLM inference, and compute-bound tasks
